scenarios.description: parseInput(String,baseUri) returns non-empty Document for simple HTML string
scenarios.expectedResult:
return Document with body containing one

element whose text is 'Hello'
pseudocode: Document doc = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()).parseInput(html, baseUri);

scenarios.description: parseInput(String,baseUri) returns empty Document for empty HTML string
scenarios.expectedResult: return Document whose body has no child nodes

scenarios.description: parseInput(String,baseUri) throws NullPointerException when html string is null
parseInput(String,baseUri) accepts null baseUri and yields Document with null baseUri
scenarios.expectedResult: return Document whose baseUri is null
pseudocode: Document doc = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()).parseInput(html, baseUri);

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader,baseUri) returns Document for StringReader input
scenarios.expectedResult: return Document with body containing <span> tag
pseudocode: Reader reader = new StringReader("Foo"); String baseUri = "http://test";

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader,baseUri) returns empty Document for Reader with no content
scenarios.expectedResult: return Document whose body has zero children
pseudocode: Reader reader = new StringReader(""); String baseUri = "";
Document doc = new Parser(new HtmlTreeBuilder()).parseInput(reader, baseUri);

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader,baseUri) throws UncheckedIOException when Reader.read throws IOException
scenarios.precondition: Reader stub throws IOException on read to simulate I/O error
pseudocode: Reader reader = new Reader(){ public int read(char[] cbuf,int off,int len)throws IOException{ throw new IOException("fail"); }}; String baseUri = "u";

scenarios.description: parseInput(String,baseUri) propagates RuntimeException thrown by TreeBuilder.parse
scenarios.precondition: Use fake TreeBuilder that throws IllegalStateException in parse()
pseudocode: TreeBuilder fake = new TreeBuilder(){ public Document parse(Reader in,String uri,Parser p){ throw new IllegalStateException("oops"); } public ParseSettings defaultSettings(){return new ParseSettings();} public TreeBuilder newInstance(){return this;} };

scenarios.description: parseInput(String,baseUri) on XmlTreeBuilder returns Document with XML namespace preserved
scenarios.expectedResult: return Document whose documentElement namespaceURI equals Parser.NamespaceXml
pseudocode: Parser parser = Parser.xmlParser(); String xml = "<root xmlns='http://ns'></root>"; String baseUri = "u";

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader,baseUri) with long HTML containing multiple siblings returns all nodes
scenarios.expectedResult: return Document whose body has 3 child <li> elements in order 1,2,3
pseudocode:
String html = "
  • 1
  • 2
  • 3
"; Reader reader = new StringReader(html); String baseUri="b";
List<Element> items = doc.body().select("li"); assert items.size() == 3 && items.get(0).text().equals("1") && items.get(2).text().equals("3");
